title: Treehouse Of Horror Xxiv

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer Simpson's signature frustrated exclamation
jokes.punchline: The iconic catchphrase opens the Halloween episode
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase, perfect economy and character integration, but completely expected

jokes.text: 'Twas Halloween night, with the kids door-to-dooring, and all over town the blood sugar was soaring.
jokes.setup: Dr. Seuss-style poetry opening
jokes.punchline: 'Blood sugar soaring' as a consequence of trick-or-treating
jokes.explanation: Clever Dr. Seuss parody with medical humor twist on Halloween candy consumption

jokes.text: But the Simpson abode was deep down in the dumps, 'cause from little to bigger, the kids had the mumps.
jokes.setup: Family is sad for unknown reason
jokes.punchline: They can't trick-or-treat because of mumps
jokes.explanation: Solid Dr. Seuss parody establishing the premise for the Fat in the Hat story

jokes.text: Now, I'm off to a party, my outfit is chic. It's a Catwoman costume, I'm sure is unique.
jokes.setup: Marge claims her Catwoman costume is unique
jokes.punchline: The irony that Catwoman is an extremely common Halloween costume
jokes.explanation: Relatable humor about common costume choices, delivered in Seussian rhyme

jokes.text: Wait a second-- it's Halloween?
jokes.setup: Halloween preparations happening all around
jokes.punchline: Homer is completely oblivious to what day it is
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer Simpson obliviousness, very economical delivery

jokes.text: Can we have some candy? Just one M&M.
jokes.setup: Kids asking for Halloween candy
jokes.punchline: Scaling down request to just one piece shows their desperation
jokes.explanation: Cute child logic, but fairly predictable humor

jokes.text: The Fat in the Hat!
jokes.setup: Cat in the Hat parody expected
jokes.punchline: Homer as 'Fat in the Hat' - perfect character substitution
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character-specific parody title that perfectly captures both Homer and the source material

jokes.text: I'll save Halloween for you three grumpy tots. Just stick out your rumps for some mump-stumping shots!
jokes.setup: Homer offering to help with Halloween
jokes.punchline: Mump shots delivered to the rear end in Seussian language
jokes.explanation: Creative Seussian wordplay with medical absurdity, though slightly forced

jokes.text: And you should be dead, you're so wrinkled and gray!
jokes.setup: Lisa confronting the suspicious Fat in the Hat
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest assessment of Homer's appearance
jokes.explanation: Dark humor from Lisa, perfectly delivered with child-like directness

jokes.text: I'll give you the business, you yellow sea cow!
jokes.setup: Grampa threatening Homer
jokes.punchline: 'Yellow sea cow' as an insult referencing Simpson skin color
jokes.explanation: Classic Grampa Simpson old-timey insult with meta humor about character design

jokes.text: I... uh, I knew there was something I left off my list.
jokes.setup: Homer realizes he forgot something important for his magical helpers
jokes.punchline: His casual reaction to a major oversight
jokes.explanation: Homer's casual incompetence, but somewhat weak delivery

jokes.text: I am the Borax. I speak for the woods. But I've plastered my likeness on consumer goods. Sellout!
jokes.setup: Lorax parody about speaking for nature
jokes.punchline: Immediately admits to selling out for commercial products
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of Dr. Seuss commercialization with perfect self-aware punchline

jokes.text: Who buzzed the buzzard? Don't pester the rich. I don't hand out candy, you son of a Gritch.
jokes.setup: Mr. Burns answering door to trick-or-treaters
jokes.punchline: 'Son of a Gritch' combining Grinch with profanity substitute
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay combining Dr. Seuss with profanity, perfect for Burns character

jokes.text: Enough with the lessons! This isn't a school!
jokes.setup: Dr. Seuss stories typically have moral lessons
jokes.punchline: Bart directly addressing and rejecting the educational aspect
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about children's literature conventions, perfectly delivered by Bart

jokes.text: I don't got no candy, I only serve beer. And who said that you could bring minors in here?
jokes.setup: Moe confronting trick-or-treaters
jokes.punchline: Treating children like unwelcome bar patrons
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe characterization treating kids like bar customers

jokes.text: And you just pissed off the wrong fat, furry drunk!
jokes.setup: Barney being insulted
jokes.punchline: Self-aware description while threatening violence
jokes.explanation: Barney's self-aware insult works well with character

jokes.text: This is supposed to be a children's story!
jokes.setup: Violence escalating in the Dr. Seuss parody
jokes.punchline: Lisa breaking fourth wall about inappropriate content
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-humor acknowledging the tonal shift from children's story

jokes.setup: Apu being threatened with babysitting eight kids
jokes.punchline: His horrified reaction to the punishment
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective reaction to parenting nightmare scenario

jokes.text: I'm frightened of nothing, not even hellfires. Just don't let me ever be played by Mike Myers.
jokes.setup: Homer claiming fearlessness
jokes.punchline: His one fear is being portrayed by Mike Myers (Cat in the Hat movie)
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke referencing the widely panned Mike Myers Cat in the Hat film

jokes.text: What the hell?!
jokes.setup: Transitioning between stories abruptly
jokes.punchline: Marge's confused reaction to sudden story shift
jokes.explanation: Basic transition reaction, nothing particularly clever

jokes.text: Hey, if they get on an airbus, they know they're taking their chances.
jokes.setup: Warning about flying kite near airport
jokes.punchline: Bart's callous attitude toward passenger safety
jokes.explanation: Dark humor typical of Bart's mischievous character

jokes.text: I don't know what that means, I have a digital watch!
jokes.setup: Air traffic controller using clock position terminology
jokes.punchline: Homer's literal misunderstanding of aviation terminology
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic failure with great modern tech reference

jokes.text: Homina, homina, homina. Over.
jokes.setup: Controller sees Marge's photo
jokes.punchline: Jackie Gleason reference while maintaining radio protocol
jokes.explanation: Classic reference humor with professional protocol absurdity

jokes.text: I'm seeing a 12-car pileup, a naked rooftop cookout, and... Oh, my God! A box kite!
jokes.setup: Reporter listing increasingly serious incidents
jokes.punchline: Treating a harmless box kite as the most shocking
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation subversion - innocuous item treated as most shocking

jokes.text: I'm alive! All patched up! End of story. Actually, there's a little more.
jokes.setup: Bart declaring everything is fine
jokes.punchline: Immediate contradiction that there's more to the story
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection setup for the body-sharing reveal

jokes.text: Strangle me out of this, Fatso.
jokes.setup: Bart attached to Lisa's body
jokes.punchline: Requesting his usual punishment as escape method
jokes.explanation: Clever inversion of the classic Homer-Bart strangling dynamic

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer realizes he made a mistake
jokes.punchline: Classic Homer exclamation
jokes.explanation: Another iconic catchphrase moment

jokes.text: Man cave! Everyone's welcome to my man cave!
jokes.setup: Homer excited about having his own space
jokes.punchline: Immediately defeats purpose by inviting everyone
jokes.explanation: Homer's social nature undermining his desire for solitude

jokes.text: Derivative and repetitive! F-minus.
jokes.setup: Bart's show-and-tell about being attached to Lisa
jokes.punchline: Harsh academic critique of his medical condition
jokes.explanation: Absurd academic standards applied to medical emergency

jokes.text: It's like looking down a Canadian tunnel.
jokes.setup: Commenting on Lisa's clean ears
jokes.punchline: Bizarre comparison to Canadian infrastructure
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully random and specific comparison that feels very Bart-like

jokes.text: Well, that's for lettuce.
jokes.setup: Two heads for one coupon
jokes.punchline: Coupon is for produce, not medical conditions
jokes.explanation: Absurd literal interpretation of promotional coupon

jokes.text: Should've chopped off Bart's head long ago.
jokes.setup: Homer observing the two-headed situation
jokes.punchline: Casual suggestion of extreme violence toward his son
jokes.explanation: Dark Homer parenting humor, delivered with characteristic casualness

jokes.text: 'Cause in a head-to-head battle, the one with the most brains wins!
jokes.setup: Lisa and Bart fighting while sharing a body
jokes.punchline: Literal 'head-to-head' battle with brains reference
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that works on multiple levels with the attached heads premise

jokes.text: ♪ Mock... Yeah. ♪ Ing... Yeah.
jokes.setup: Forced to sing karaoke duets in hell
jokes.punchline: Reference to earlier mockumentary joke
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier joke but with diminishing returns

jokes.text: Seriously, folks, I really think we should stop this Hitler guy.
jokes.setup: 1930s circus performance
jokes.punchline: Krusty breaking character with modern historical perspective
jokes.explanation: Excellent anachronistic humor with Krusty's modern awareness in period setting

jokes.text: So, uh, anyone here from New Jersey? I'm goin' there next week.
jokes.setup: Moe as circus freak being displayed
jokes.punchline: Doing standard stand-up crowd work while being exhibited
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of freak show dynamics with modern comedy club behavior

jokes.text: I am so sick and tired of people assuming that the human snail is, in some way, slow.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy as human snail complaining about assumptions
jokes.punchline: Being offended by accurate stereotype while proving it true
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy pedantry applied to his physical transformation, excellent character integration

jokes.text: I, too, am a freak. One eye is blue, and the other a pale brown.
jokes.setup: Marge claiming to be a freak to fit in
jokes.punchline: Her 'deformity' is extremely minor eye color difference
jokes.explanation: Sweet Marge moment trying to relate, with typical minor 'flaw'

jokes.text: Of all the kisses I have ever gotten in my life, that was the first.
jokes.setup: Moe describing a meaningful kiss
jokes.punchline: It's literally the only kiss he's ever received
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe loneliness humor with excellent misdirection structure

jokes.text: She also acquired it on her deathbed. That was a very busy deathbed.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining his mother gave him a ring on her deathbed
jokes.punchline: She stole it while dying, making death productive
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation with 'very busy deathbed' - excellent dark humor with perfect timing

jokes.text: What do you know? I am gathering moss!
jokes.setup: Homer rolling down a hill
jokes.punchline: Literal application of 'rolling stone gathers no moss' saying
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay on common saying, functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: What the hell does 'gooble goo' mean? We don't know! Gooble goo!
jokes.setup: Circus freaks chanting their signature phrase
jokes.punchline: Admitting they don't understand their own chant
jokes.explanation: Self-aware humor about meaningless group chants, good meta-commentary

jokes.text: This is exactly why circus attendance is plummeting!
jokes.setup: Violent mob scene at circus
jokes.punchline: Blaming it on business concerns
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary on entertainment industry problems

jokes.text: This is exactly why circus attendance is plummeting!
jokes.setup: Chaos and violence at the circus
jokes.punchline: Homer's practical business analysis of the situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer observation about modern entertainment decline during chaos

jokes.text: And that, kids, is how I met your mother.
jokes.setup: End of bizarre circus story
jokes.punchline: Reference to 'How I Met Your Mother' framing device
jokes.explanation: Solid meta-reference that frames the entire story

tmdb_overview: The scariest Simpsons Halloween show yet, featuring an opening by Guillermo Del Toro.
